tool_args.label: Original 2020-2024 Capital Program Approval
tool_args.raw_text: The original $54.799 billion MTA 2020-2024 Capital Program included $51.472 billion for CPRB agencies and $3.327 billion for the self-funded Bridges and Tunnels (B&T) program... was approved by the MTA Board on September 25, 2019.

payload.description: Original $54.799 billion MTA 2020-2024 Capital Program approved by MTA Board

evidence_refs.source_quote: approved by the MTA Board on September 25, 2019

tool_args.label: Amendment #1 CPRB Approval
tool_args.raw_text: The CPRB approved this amendment on December 23, 2021.

payload.description: Amendment #1 approved by CPRB, increasing Capital Program by $535 million from $54.799B to $55.334B

evidence_refs.source_quote: CPRB approved this amendment on December 23, 2021

tool_args.label: Amendment #2 CPRB Approval

tool_args.raw_text: The CPRB approved this amendment on July 31, 2023.

tool_args.label: Amendment #4 MTA Board Approval
tool_args.raw_text: Amendment #4: On October 30, 2024, the MTA Board approved a letter amendment (Amendment #4) to the 2020-2024 Capital Program. This amendment only impacted Network Expansion budgets, increasing the Network Expansion budget from $10.291 billion to $10.412 billion.

payload.description: Amendment #4 (letter amendment) approved, increased Network Expansion budget from $10.291B to $10.412B

evidence_refs.source_quote: Amendment #4: On October 30, 2024, the MTA Board approved a letter amendment (Amendment #4)

tool_args.label: Purchase 475 Battery-Electric Buses
tool_args.raw_text: The increase in the Buses category reflects higher costs for the rollout of battery-electric buses, including an increase in the projects to purchase 475 battery-electric buses and an increase in the project to deploy electric bus charging infrastructure.

payload.description: Net increase of +$22 million based on bid results for purchase of 475 battery-electric buses, split into Purchase 193 Standard Electric Buses and Purchase 72 Articulated Electric Buses

evidence_refs.source_quote: increase in the projects to purchase 475 battery-electric buses
There is a net increase in the projects to purchase 475 battery-electric buses (+$22 million)

tool_args.label: Platform Barriers Initiative (NYCT)
tool_args.raw_text: A new Platform Barriers project (+$45 million). The new Platform Barriers project will install static platform barriers at select stations systemwide, thereby enhancing station security more quickly and efficiently and at a greater number of stations.

payload.description: New safety-focused platform barrier initiative to install static platform barriers at select stations systemwide, enhancing station security more quickly and efficiently

evidence_refs.source_quote: A new Platform Barriers project (+$45 million). The new Platform Barriers project will install static platform barriers at select stations systemwide

tool_args.label: Throgs Neck Bridge Safety Fence and Cable Dehumidification
tool_args.raw_text: The primary focus of Amendment #5 consists of the addition of a new project to install a safety fence on the Throgs Neck Bridge. ... another new project to support preliminary design for main cable dehumidification at the Throgs Neck Bridge will also be added

payload.project_name: Throgs Neck Bridge Safety Fence and Cable Dehumidification
payload.description: New project to install a safety fence on the Throgs Neck Bridge and new project for preliminary design for main cable dehumidification at the Throgs Neck Bridge

evidence_refs.source_quote: addition of a new project to install a safety fence on the Throgs Neck Bridge
another new project to support preliminary design for main cable dehumidification at the Throgs Neck Bridge

tool_args.label: Purchase 92 Express Buses
tool_args.raw_text: There is an increase in the Purchase 92 Express Buses project (+$47.2 million), based on the most recent estimates. There is also an increase in the Bus Purchase Design project (+$0.9 million), mainly due to design costs for the 92 Express Buses.

payload.description: Increase of +$47.2 million based on the most recent estimates

evidence_refs.source_quote: increase in the Purchase 92 Express Buses project (+$47.2 million), based on the most recent estimates

tool_args.label: ADA Stations Accessibility Update
tool_args.raw_text: Accessibility projects at four stations are added to this capital program: Fort Hamilton Parkway station on the West End line, 2nd Avenue station on the 6th Ave line, and Morrison Avenue-Soundview and Middletown Road stations on the Pelham line. Accessibility projects at two stations, which were previously included in this capital program, are now delayed to a future capital program due to technical complexities encountered during the scoping process: Kings Highway station on the Sea Beach line and 168 Street on the Broadway-7th Ave line. The total number of stations being made accessible in this capital program is now 69.

payload.claim_text: Four stations added to accessibility program: Fort Hamilton Parkway (West End line), 2nd Avenue (6th Ave line), Morrison Avenue-Soundview and Middletown Road (Pelham line). Two stations delayed: Kings Highway (Sea Beach line) and 168 Street (Broadway-7th Ave line). Total stations being made accessible now 69.
